title: Minor Mosque
name: ismail Kocabaş
text:
Minor Somsa'nın hemen yanında bulunan bu camiyi (Minor Camii), somsa keyfinizin ardından ziyaret etmenizi kesinlikle öneririm.

Caminin tasarımı oldukça sade, duru ve basit bir güzelliğe sahip. Bu minimal ve ferahlatıcı görünüm, insanın içini anında huzurla dolduruyor. Detaylarda boğulmadan, sadece birkaç dakika içinde gezebileceğiniz ve ruhunuzu dinlendirebileceğiniz, kesinlikle görülmeye değer bir yapı.

Sade mimarinin ruhunuzu dinlendirmesini sevenler için harika bir durak!

I highly recommend visiting this mosque (Minor Mosque), located right next to Minor Somsa, after enjoying your somsa.

The mosque's design possesses a beauty that is quite simple, pure, and serene. This minimal and refreshing appearance instantly fills one's soul with peace. It is a structure definitely worth seeing; you can tour it and soothe your spirit within just a few minutes, without being overwhelmed by details.

A wonderful stop for those who appreciate simple architecture that calms the soul!

title: Minor Mosque
name: Yo’lovchi!
text:
Minor Mosque – A Peaceful Gem in Tashkent

The Minor Mosque is one of the most beautiful and serene places to visit in Tashkent. Located near the Ankhor River, this stunning white marble mosque is a relatively modern structure, completed in 2014, yet it carries the elegance and peace of classical Islamic architecture. The exterior is breathtaking with its tall white minarets and large turquoise dome, which shine beautifully under sunlight or in the evening lights.

Inside, the prayer hall is spacious, clean, and warmly welcoming. The atmosphere is calm and spiritual, making it perfect for reflection, prayer, or just peaceful observation. The air-conditioning makes it very comfortable even during summer. Non-Muslim visitors are also welcome and often amazed by the detailed interior designs and calligraphy.

The mosque is well maintained, with clean ablution areas and organized entry. It’s also wheelchair accessible. The surrounding area is beautifully landscaped and ideal for photography or a quiet walk.

Minor Mosque is not only a place of worship but also a symbol of modern Islamic culture in Uzbekistan. Whether you’re a local, a tourist, or simply someone looking for a tranquil spot in the city, Minor Mosque is definitely worth visiting.

⭐ Highly recommended for peaceful vibes and cultural beauty.

title: Minor Mosque
name: Kaif Kamran
text:
The Minor Mosque is one of the most beautiful and peaceful places to visit in Tashkent. Its stunning white marble architecture shines brilliantly under the sunlight, giving it a calm and majestic presence. The mosque is modern yet built in a traditional Islamic style, offering a perfect mix of elegance and spirituality.

It is located beside a serene canal and surrounded by a large, lush garden that’s full of greenery and very well maintained. The clean and peaceful environment adds to the overall experience.

I had the opportunity to pray Juma Namaz (Friday prayer) here, and it was a spiritually fulfilling experience. The mosque is spacious and well-organized, making it a comfortable place for worshippers.

It’s especially beautiful during sunset when the mosque takes on a golden glow. Easily accessible by metro or taxi, the Minor Mosque is a must-visit for those interested in architecture, culture, or simply seeking peace.

title: Minor Mosque
name: Rahul Narrane
text: The Minor Mosque is one of Tashkent’s most beautiful and serene landmarks. Clad in white marble with a striking blue dome, it stands gracefully along the Ankhor River, offering a calm and inviting atmosphere. Its elegant architecture, intricate details, and spacious courtyard make it a perfect place for quiet reflection or a peaceful stroll. Whether you’re visiting for spiritual reasons or simply to admire its beauty, the mosque leaves a lasting impression.

title: Minor Mosque
name: Wasim Akhtar
text: The Minor Mosque, located along the Ankhor River in Tashkent, is a modern architectural gem and an important spiritual landmark. Built in 2014, it stands out with its serene white marble façade, elegant blue dome, and peaceful ambiance. Though newer than many historic mosques, it symbolizes Uzbekistan’s post-independence religious revival. The mosque welcomes both worshippers and tourists, offering a tranquil space for reflection. Its contemporary design harmoniously blends tradition with modernity, making it a must-visit cultural and spiritual site in the city.

There is a cemetery past that garden. While the interior of the masjid is on the smaller size, it is beautifully decorated with calligraphy and setpieces. The surrounding area is lively and has many places to visit for shopping and dining after a visit to this masjid. There is also a women's prayer area.

text: Мечеть Минор впечатляет не только снаружи, но и внутри. Когда заходишь внутрь, сразу ощущаешь величие и духовную чистоту этого места. Просторный молитвенный зал оформлен в светлых тонах, что создаёт ощущение легкости и покоя. Высокие потолки, изящные узоры, мраморные колонны и красивый купол с орнаментами — всё сделано со вкусом и благоговением. Внутри очень светло, благодаря большим окнам и умелой подсветке. Акустика потрясающая — голос имама разносится чётко и мягко. Это место, где хочется задержаться, чтобы почувствовать внутреннюю гармонию.

title: Minor Mosque
name: Andranik Mesropyan
text:
Мечеть Минор в Ташкенте – символ современного исламского зодчества

Мечеть Минор – одна из самых красивых и величественных достопримечательностей Ташкента. Несмотря на свою относительную молодость (она была открыта в 2014 году), Минор уже стала важным символом столицы и местом притяжения как для верующих, так и для туристов.

Мечеть поражает своей элегантной архитектурой: белоснежный фасад, изящные голубые купола и два стройных минарета высотой 42 метра создают ощущение лёгкости и гармонии. Внутреннее убранство выполнено в традиционном восточном стиле с великолепной резьбой, каллиграфией и мозаикой. Просторный молитвенный зал может вместить тысячи человек, а его сводчатый потолок украшен тончайшими узорами, создавая атмосферу умиротворения.

Мечеть расположена на берегу канала Анхор, что придаёт ей особую живописность. Вечером, когда включается подсветка, Минор выглядит особенно величественно. Это место не только для молитвы, но и для духовного уединения, где можно насладиться тишиной и красотой исламской архитектуры.

Посещение Минорской мечети оставляет незабываемые впечатления. Это одно из тех мест, где ощущаешь гармонию, спокойствие и величие восточной культуры. Рекомендуем к посещению всем гостям Ташкента!

title: Minor Mosque
name: 长脸张少侠
text:
塔什干【白色清真寺】
Minor Mosque
Minor Masjidi

清真寺于 2013 年夏天开始修建，它于 2014 年 10 月 1 日开斋节前夕开放，并继承了东方建筑的优良传统。同时，小清真寺不同于老式砖砌清真寺，采用白色大理石装饰。它在晴朗的天空下闪闪发光，其绿松石色的圆顶似乎消失在天空中。

可容纳 2400 多人。小清真寺分为带露台的开放式前部和带镀金米哈拉布（清真寺墙壁上的半圆形壁龛，指示朝拜方向（麦加天房的方向，穆斯林祈祷时应面向的方向））的大圆形大厅，装饰有《古兰经》的文字。

地址：87PG+42F, Kichik Xalqa Yo'li, Тоshkent,
时间：24小时
门票：免费

⚠️进大门到院内后，左边是女士可以进的，右边才是男士可以进的，中间是旅游团进的

title: Minor Mosque
name: Ilhomjon Dadajonov
text:
Minor masjidi — Toshkent shahridagi zamonaviy masjidlardan biri bo‘lib, uning qurilishi 2013-yilda boshlangan va 2014-yilning oktyabr oyida foydalanishga topshirilgan. Ushbu masjid Toshkentning yangi arxitektura durdonalaridan biri hisoblanadi va zamonaviy uslubda barpo etilgan bo‘lsa-da, unda islomiy an’analar ham mujassam.

Minor masjidi asosan juma va boshqa diniy namozlar uchun mo‘ljallangan. U Toshkent shahar markazida, Anhor kanali yaqinida joylashgan bo‘lib, uning oq marmardan qurilgan go‘zal binosi masjidga o‘ziga xos ko‘rk bag‘ishlaydi. Masjidning ikkita ulkan minorasi va katta moviy gumbazi shahar siluetiga yangicha nafislik qo‘shadi.

Masjidning ichki qismi keng va yorug‘ bo‘lib, u yerda bir vaqtning o‘zida minglab musulmonlar ibodat qilishi mumkin. Ichki devorlar va shiftlar islomiy naqshlar bilan bezatilgan, gilamlar va boshqa dekor elementlari esa o‘zining nozik did bilan tanlanganligi bilan ajralib turadi.

Minor masjidi Toshkent aholisi va mehmonlari uchun nafaqat ibodat joyi, balki tinchlik va ma’naviyat ramzi hamdir. Uning atrofi yashil maydonlar va xiyobonlar bilan o‘ralgan bo‘lib, mehmonlarga shinam va osoyishta muhit yaratadi. Bugungi kunda Minor masjidi Toshkentning muhim ziyoratgohlaridan biri hisoblanadi va uning go‘zalligi ko‘plab sayyohlarni o‘ziga jalb qiladi.

title: Minor Mosque
name: Balkrishna Shroff
text: It was opened in October 2014 on the eve of Eid. It's construction was financed by state budget and Spiritual Administration of Muslims in Uzbekistan. It is located on the bank of Anhor channel with beautiful landscaped surrounding. It can accept 24oo people at a time to make it one of the largest mosque in Uzbekistan. It has two minarets and dome in style of blue skyes. Internal part was decorated with naqshes while mihrab was decorated with verses from Koran and hadis. There is a special room for abulution with modern equipments. Not to missed a beautiful modern mosque in it's marbled glory...

title: Minor Mosque
name: Dejan Braki
text:
Minor Mosque is a sparkling new Mosque set in the northeastern part of the capital, close to Tashkent TV tower.
When looking from an elevated position by the main street passing by it, you can notice its grandeur and beauty. The entire Mosque is covered in shiny white marble with beautiful blue decorations at the entering muqarnas, as well two Minarets and a huge turquoise blue dome above the main praying area, resembling the traditional Uzbek style. Aside is a huge parking lot.
You can enter the Mosque for free even as a non Muslim, however cannot enter the main praying hall, but observe it from the visitors door by it which was quite enough to see the interior beauty.

Pro TIP: after visiting the Mosque head to the next door "Minor Somsa" eatery (100 m to the right) for amazing fresh baked somsa pastry

Белая мечеть, Или Минор мечеть. Очень красивая и чистая. Архитектура и дизайн на высоком уровне. Внутри все спокойно.

title: Minor Mosque
name: Shohimardon Ibragimov
text: Minor masjidi — Toshkent shahrida yangi qurilgan masjid[1] Masjidning qurilish ishlari 2013-yil yoz oylarida boshlangan. 2014-yilning 1-oktyabrida, musulmonlarning katta Qurbon hayiti bayramida Oʻzbekiston Prezidenti Islom Karimov yangi Minor masjidini ochib berdi. Masjid binosi anʼanaviy sharq va oʻzbek uslubida qurilgan boʻlib, ikki minorasi va osmonrang bir gumbaz bor. Masjidning ichi esa naqsh tarzida bezatilgan. Masjid 2400 dan ortiq odamlar uchun moʻljallangan.

title: Minor Mosque
name: Louis L.
text:
This mosque was really beautiful and different from the other Mosques we’ve seen in Uzbekistan. Interior was really beautiful and the courtyard too. Must visit if you’re in Tashkent.

AND GUYS ! Use Yandex app in Uzbekistan it’s super cheap and convenient to move around. Prices probably 300% lower than normal taxis

Also, women should wear appropriate clothes while visiting the mosque.
